title: Country Ambassadors
subtitle: Country-level outreach, translation, and local relationship-building
summary: A volunteer-first ambassador model for local outreach, translation, relationship-building, and support documentation.
synopsis: Country ambassadors can give Sutton SignWriting trusted local help with introductions, translation, context, documentation, and follow-up while staying inside clear role boundaries. This entry defines the role, what ambassadors do, what they do not do, how a volunteer-first pilot can work, and how future stipends might be handled responsibly.

slides.title: Why The Role Is Needed
slides.summary: Local context matters, and international outreach cannot be centralized well without trusted country-level help.

image_prompts.concrete: World map slide with country-level connectors linking local SignWriting communities, schools, universities, and documentation work
image_prompts.abstract: Distributed local connectors extending a shared stewardship network

slides.speech: Country-level outreach is hard to run well from one central point. Local language, culture, institutions, communities, and follow-up patterns matter.

slides.title: Clear Role Boundaries
slides.summary: Ambassadors are local connectors, not legal, financial, or institutional representatives.

image_prompts.concrete: Role boundary slide separating local introductions, translation, context, and follow-up from legal, financial, and institutional authority
image_prompts.abstract: A clear boundary line around trusted local coordination

bullets: No formal university representation unless authorized
slides.speech: The role works only if its boundaries are clear. Ambassadors help with local relationships and communication. They do not make legal or financial commitments or represent an institution unless explicitly authorized.

slides.title: Volunteer-First Pilot
slides.summary: Start with a small pilot, shared materials, visible recognition, and clear follow-up.

image_prompts.concrete: Pilot program slide showing three to five country nodes, shared materials, simple tracking, visible recognition, and review before expansion
image_prompts.abstract: A small pilot structure designed to learn before scaling

slides.speech: The model should begin as a small volunteer-first pilot in three to five countries, with shared materials, simple tracking, visible recognition, and enough structure to learn before expanding.

slides.title: Recognition And Stipends
slides.summary: If funding becomes available, stipends should be modest, transparent, bounded, and tied to specific coordination work.

image_prompts.concrete: Country ambassador stewardship slide showing volunteer recognition, transparent stipend criteria, bounded tasks, and accountable coordination
image_prompts.abstract: Recognition and modest compensation held inside clear stewardship boundaries

slides.speech: Recognition should be part of the model from the beginning. If funding later makes stipends possible, they should be modest, transparent, and tied to bounded coordination work. Compensation should not imply legal, financial, or institutional authority.
slides.notes: Close by making future compensation responsible rather than vague.
